purpose: Descriptive syntactic-SHAPE profile: the DISTRIBUTION of dependency distances (the linear span of each syntactic link, d = |i - head.i|) — the histogram, the adjacent-link share (d=1), and the long-range tail (d>=threshold). The scalar MDD (per-sentence mean/SD) already ships as variance_audit.mdd_stats and is REUSED here, not re-implemented; the distribution is the additive contribution (arXiv:2211.14620). Parser-tier (reuses the shared spaCy pipeline); abstains (available:false / missing_dependency) without it. NOT a verdict: not authorship/AI, not a quality/readability score, and NOT length-controlled (MDD covaries with sentence length; mean_sentence_length co-reported). Claim-license refuses those inferences; thresholds operator-side / PROVISIONAL.
use_when: You want a descriptive syntactic-complexity profile (how long-range a writer's dependencies run)
You want the dependency-distance distribution, not just the mean (which mdd_stats already gives)
Target is English, >= ~150 words, and a dependency parser is installed
do_not_use_when: No spaCy / en_core_web_sm available (the surface abstains)
You want an authorship/AI verdict or a writing-quality judgment
Non-English text (MDD norms are language-specific), or a length-controlled reading

compute.cost_note: spaCy dependency parse (CPU); reuses the shared variance_audit._NLP pipeline.

references: The distribution of syntactic dependency distances (arXiv:2211.14620): https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.14620
